message:

 {"id":"bd-1v2bs","title":"Add unit tests for ftui-text Unicode normalization module","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanBear","created_at":"2026-02-09T18:04:00.707814001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:05:33.003013765Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T18:05:33.002988889Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1vb18","title":"Fix emoji-triggered border corruption in demo showcase (keep emojis)","description":"Problem
- Demo showcase has border misalignment/corruption in File Browser and Markdown screens when emojis are present. Emojis must remain.

Likely Causes
1) Several screens try to "clear" the full area using Paragraph::new("") + bg style, but Paragraph only paints style and does not clear cell content. With Flex gaps between panels, stale border characters from prior frames/screens can bleed into those gaps and appear as misaligned borders.
2) Buffer::print_text_clipped (ftui-render) advances by grapheme_width() but may only write the first char for multi-codepoint graphemes (e.g. VS16 sequences like "⚙️"), leaving holes that can preserve stale border chars.

Fix
- Clear demo screen root areas with actual space cells (bg=surface) so gaps are deterministically blank.
- Make Buffer::print_text_clipped fill any extra width when falling back to first char so no holes remain (and add a regression test).

Acceptance
- File Browser and Markdown screens render without stray/misaligned borders in gaps.
- Emojis remain enabled in file browser icons and unicode showcase.
- cargo fmt/check/clippy/test pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:01:09.704771751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706728130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706701631Z","close_reason":"Completed (emoji border corruption fixed: VS16 width policy + buffer filler + empty paragraph clears)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["borders","demo-showcase","emoji","render","text"]}
-{"id":"bd-1vb19","title":"task(validation): build user-visible benchmark gates for runtime shell and doctor workflows","description":"Background:
Benchmarking needs to measure things that matter to users and operators, not just raw throughput. The migration should be judged on responsiveness, shutdown, cancellation, and doctor turnaround quality.

Goal:
Build the benchmark and regression-gate suite for user-visible/runtime-visible outcomes.","design":"Work packages:
- Define benchmarks for effect turnaround, subscription responsiveness, shutdown latency, child-process cancellation, and doctor workflow wall-clock.
- Capture baseline and post-change evidence across both canonical user journeys and challenge scenarios that stress degraded-mode, recovery, and failure-heavy paths.
- Emit human-readable and machine-readable reports.
- Wire the metrics into rollout scoring.
- Make explicit which user-visible outcomes must improve broadly and which may only hold as graceful-fallback guarantees under challenge conditions.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Benchmarks are tied to named user/operator outcomes from the invariant bead.
2. Reports highlight regressions and improvements clearly.
3. Benchmark artifacts are detailed enough to support triage and release decisions.
4. The benchmark gate explicitly checks that claimed wins are not confined to narrow curated scenarios and that challenge-path user outcomes remain acceptable.","notes":"Avoid vanity metrics. A smaller internal number matters only if it maps to a better experience or stronger safety margin.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:33:26.816650187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T00:55:01.771803424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync-frankentui","benchmark","performance","validation"]}

A smaller internal number matters only if it maps to a better experience or stronger safety margin.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:33:26.816650187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T00:59:25.644407153Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T00:59:25.643882471Z","close_reason":"Runtime benchmark baselines added to tests/baseline.json (shutdown_latency, first_frame, command_roundtrip, effect_queue_drain) with p50/p95/p99/p999 budgets in ns. BenchmarkGate tests prove: baselines load, pass within budget, fail on regression, produce readable summaries. 4 new tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync-frankentui","benchmark","performance","validation"]}

 {"id":"bd-1w0w4","title":"feature(pane-rollout): docs, examples, migration, and release gates","description":"Goal\n- Make the pane system production-adoptable with clear docs and guarded rollout.\n\nWhat this feature owns\n- API docs, cookbook examples, and migration guidance.\n- Demo showcase scenarios emphasizing pane workflows.\n- Release checklist and go/no-go quality gates.\n\nDefinition of done\n- Users can adopt pane workspace APIs with minimal confusion.\n- Release gate checklist is explicit, enforceable, and automated where possible.","design":"Rollout philosophy\n- Deliver adoption-ready APIs and docs only after validation evidence exists.\n- Showcase acts as both user-facing proof and regression sentinel.\n- Release gates are objective and automated where feasible to prevent subjective ship decisions.","acceptance_criteria":"1) API/docs/showcase/migration artifacts are complete and internally consistent.\n2) Rollout checklist references concrete validation/perf/a11y evidence, not subjective confidence.\n3) Operators have incident/triage runbooks grounded in test observability artifacts.\n4) Release is blocked when any mandatory quality gate fails.","notes":"Execution lane: Track F (rollout/integration).\nPrimary sequence: bd-1w0w4.1 -> {bd-1w0w4.2, bd-1w0w4.3, bd-1w0w4.4} -> bd-1w0w4.7 -> bd-1w0w4.5 -> bd-1w0w4.6.\nUser impact: safe, well-documented rollout with objective go/no-go gates and reproducible evidence package.\nEvidence required: traceability matrix links + release-evidence bundle + gate pass/fail artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":1320,"created_at":"2026-02-11T05:30:51.281448949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:17:20.808479815Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","docs","pane","rollout","terminal","ux","wasm","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4","depends_on_id":"bd-16jx8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4","depends_on_id":"bd-1qkzq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4","depends_on_id":"bd-21pbi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4","depends_on_id":"bd-a46q1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4","depends_on_id":"bd-gajnk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4","depends_on_id":"bd-x9lqw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1w0w4.1","title":"task(pane-rollout): finalize pane workspace public API surface and stability contract","description":"Background\n- A powerful pane system only succeeds if the exposed API is coherent, minimal-surprise, and future-resilient.\n\nScope\n- Review and finalize public types/functions/events for pane workspace usage in both terminal and web integrations.\n- Define explicit stability boundaries, extension points, and intentionally-internal APIs.\n- Ensure naming and semantics align with existing ftui design principles (determinism, correctness, layered architecture).\n\nAcceptance\n- Public API contract is explicit, internally consistent, and reviewed against real integration scenarios.\n- No critical ambiguity remains around ownership/lifecycle/state responsibilities.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Deliver scoped rollout artifact with precise, implementation-backed guidance.\n2) Validate linked examples/scripts/checklists against current behavior and CI workflows.\n3) Ensure references to tests, diagnostics, and gates are concrete and reproducible.\n4) Include operator-facing troubleshooting guidance that references artifact bundle diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-11T05:38:01.824267063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:17:20.889254982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","docs","pane","rollout","terminal","wasm","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1efcv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1w0w4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-23pre","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w0w4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2tp51","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
